title: SIN registry entry uses inconsistent identifier or status fields (sin_id vs id, no status)
description: Canonical SIN registry entries require both a stable identifier field and a status indicator. Two schemas are in use: legacy v1 (sin_id + status:'pattern-defined' + severity:CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW) and newer v2 (id + severity:Blocking|Advisory + no status). Consumers (iter41 scoreboard generator, SIN scanner, pipeline feedback) must read both, or rows silently disappear from drilldowns and metrics. Any SIN-PATTERN-*.json that omits BOTH `sin_id` and `id`, or omits `severity`, or has neither `status` nor `is_resolved`, is non-conforming and breaks referential integrity.
detection_method: Static scan of sin_registry/*.json: parse JSON; assert presence of (sin_id OR id) AND severity AND (status OR is_resolved).
remediation: Adopt dual-key writes: every new SIN-PATTERN-*.json MUST include BOTH `id` and `sin_id` (identical values), explicit `severity`, AND either `status:'pattern-defined'` (preferred) or `is_resolved:false`. Backfill existing entries via scripts/Sync-PipelineRegistryFromDisk.ps1.
examples:
# Bad - newer file with only `id`, drops out of legacy index lookups
{ "id": "SIN-PATTERN-XXX", "severity": "Blocking" }
